data.content: Now let me read all the source files in parallel:
Now I have a complete picture of the codebase. Let me create the test project.

arguments.command: cd /tmp/sv-dfb22fb452054708bfd403cf2746c8fd && dotnet new xunit -n ContosoUniversity.Tests -o ContosoUniversity.Tests --framework net10.0 2>&1 | tail -5

data.reasoningText:
Now I have a complete understanding of the codebase. Let me create a comprehensive test project. I need to:

1. Create a test project with xUnit, Moq, and Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.InMemory
2. Write tests for:
   - PaginatedList<T>
   - NotificationService
   - Models (Person/Student/Instructor FullName, ErrorViewModel.ShowRequestId)
   - HomeController (Index, About, Contact, Error, Unauthorized)
   - StudentsController (Index with sorting/filtering/paging, Details, Create GET/POST, Edit GET/POST, Delete GET/POST)
   - CoursesController (Index, Details, Create GET/POST, Edit GET/POST, Delete GET/POST, file upload validation)
   - DepartmentsController (Index, Details, Create GET/POST, Edit GET/POST, Delete GET/POST, concurrency)
   - InstructorsController (Index, Details, Create GET/POST, Edit GET/POST, Delete GET/POST, course assignments)
   - NotificationsController (GetNotifications, MarkAsRead, Index)
   - DbInitializer

I'll use EF Core InMemory provider for tests instead of mocking the DbContext, which will give me more realistic integration testing while keeping things fast and isolated.

Now I'm setting up the test project structure with separate folders for helpers, models, services, and controllers, plus a dedicated test file for PaginatedList. Since the controllers depend directly on SchoolContext rather than an interface, I'll need to use the InMemory provider. The controllers also take dependencies like IWebHostEnvironment and ILogger that I'll mock, and I'm realizing that testing InstructorsController.Edit will be challenging since it uses TryUpdateModelAsync, which is a Controller method that's difficult to test in isolation.

I'll set up the test project using xUnit, EntityFrameworkCore.InMemory for the database, and Moq for mocking the external dependencies.
